mcqs.question: In which Java version were private interface methods introduced?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods were introduced in Java 9 as part of the Java Platform Module System (JPMS).

mcqs.question: Which of the following is TRUE about private interface methods?

options.text: They can be overridden by implementing classes

mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods must have a body (implementation) because they cannot be abstract.

mcqs.question: What is the main motivation behind introducing private interface methods in Java?

options.text: To allow multiple inheritance of state
To enable code reuse in default methods

mcqs.correctAnswer: To enable code reuse in default methods
mcqs.explanation: Private methods allow extracting common logic from multiple default methods to avoid code duplication without exposing the helper methods.

mcqs.question: Can an implementing class override a private interface method?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods are not visible to implementing classes and cannot be overridden.

mcqs.question: Which modifiers can be used together for interface methods in Java 9+?
mcqs.explanation: Java 9 allows 'private static' methods in interfaces. Private non-static methods can only be called by default methods within the interface.

mcqs.question: What is the correct syntax for a private method in an interface?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods use the 'private' keyword followed by the return type, method name, and implementation in curly braces.

mcqs.question: Can a private interface method call another private method in the same interface?
mcqs.explanation: Private methods can call other private methods within the same interface, which is a common pattern for code organization.

mcqs.question: Which type of interface method can call a private interface method?
mcqs.explanation: Only default methods and other private methods within the same interface can call private interface methods.

mcqs.question: What is the visibility scope of a private interface method?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods are accessible only within the interface where they are declared, similar to private class members.

mcqs.question: Are private interface methods part of the public API?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods are hidden implementation details and are not accessible to implementing classes or external code.

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between private and default interface methods?

options.text: Private methods can be called from outside
Default methods are inherited, private are not

mcqs.correctAnswer: Default methods are inherited, private are not
mcqs.explanation: Default methods are inherited by implementing classes and can be overridden, while private methods are internal helpers not visible to implementing classes.

mcqs.question: Can private interface methods throw checked exceptions?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods can throw checked exceptions following the same rules as other methods in Java.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT allowed for private interface methods?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods cannot be overridden by implementing classes. They are not part of the interface's contract.

mcqs.question: In an interface with default methods, when should private methods be used?

options.text: To share common logic between default methods

mcqs.correctAnswer: To share common logic between default methods
mcqs.explanation: Private methods are used to extract and share common implementation logic among multiple default methods in the same interface.

mcqs.question: What is the output of a call to a private interface method from outside the interface?
mcqs.explanation: Calling a private interface method from outside results in a compile-time error because the method is not visible.

mcqs.question: Can an interface contain both private static and private non-static methods?
mcqs.explanation: Java 9+ allows interfaces to have both private static methods (callable from any static or default method) and private non-static methods (callable from default methods).

mcqs.question: What problem did private interface methods solve that static methods could not?
mcqs.explanation: Static methods in interfaces are public and accessible outside. Private methods allow code reuse internally while keeping implementation hidden.

mcqs.question: How do private interface methods contribute to encapsulation?
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods encapsulate internal implementation details, keeping the interface's public contract clean and separate from helper logic.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is required to define a private method in an interface starting from Java 9?
mcqs.explanation: The 'private' keyword is used to define private methods in interfaces starting from Java 9.

mcqs.question: True or False: Private interface methods can be abstract.
mcqs.explanation: Private interface methods cannot be abstract because they must have a body/implementation. Only default and static methods can be abstract in interfaces.
